annotations.label: Injective function (informal)

anchor.snippet: The function is injective , or one-to-one , if each element of the codomain is mapped to by at most one element of the domain

annotations.note: Function.Injective (f a = f b → a = b) is the Lean-core definition used throughout Mathlib; Mathlib.Logic.Function.Defs builds on it.

annotations.label: Surjective function (informal)

anchor.snippet: The function is surjective , or onto , if each element of the codomain is mapped to by at least one element of the domain

annotations.note: Function.Surjective (∀ b, ∃ a, f a = b) is the Lean-core definition used throughout Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bijective function (informal)

anchor.snippet: The function is bijective ( one-to-one and onto , one-to-one correspondence , or invertible ) if each element of the codomain is mapped to by exactly one element of the domain

annotations.note: Function.Bijective is defined as Injective f ∧ Surjective f in Mathlib.Logic.Function.Defs.

annotations.label: Relation holding for any function

anchor.snippet: In any case (for any function), the following holds:

annotations.note: This is a vague meta-statement with no single corresponding declaration to formalize.

annotations.label: Injection

anchor.snippet: A function is injective ( one-to-one ) if each possible element of the codomain is mapped to by at most one argument.

annotations.note: Same Function.Injective definition (Lean core), matching the formal Injection section verbatim.

annotations.label: Injective iff left-invertible

anchor.snippet: is left- invertible

annotations.note: injective_iff_hasLeftInverse states Injective f ↔ HasLeftInverse f, verified in Mathlib.Logic.Function.Basic.

annotations.label: Injection induces bijection onto image

anchor.snippet: every injection induces a bijection onto its image

annotations.note: Equiv.ofInjective builds α ≃ range f from an injective f, the bijection onto the image.

annotations.label: Composition of injections is injective

anchor.snippet: The composition of two injections is again an injection

annotations.note: Function.Injective.comp (Lean core, used in Mathlib.Logic.Function.Defs) gives Injective g → Injective f → Injective (g ∘ f).

annotations.label: Every embedding is injective

anchor.snippet: Every embedding is injective.

annotations.note: Function.Embedding.injective extracts injectivity from the bundled embedding type in Mathlib.Logic.Embedding.Basic.

annotations.label: Surjection

anchor.snippet: A function is surjective or onto if each element of the codomain is mapped to by at least one element of the domain

annotations.note: Same Function.Surjective definition (Lean core) matching the formal Surjection section.

annotations.label: Surjective iff right-invertible

anchor.snippet: is surjective if and only if it is right-invertible

annotations.note: surjective_iff_hasRightInverse states Surjective f ↔ HasRightInverse f, verified in Mathlib.Logic.Function.Basic.

annotations.label: Surjection induces bijection from quotient

anchor.snippet: every surjection induces a bijection from a quotient set of its domain to its codomain

annotations.note: quotientKerEquivOfSurjective gives Quotient (ker f) ≃ β from a surjection, i.e. a bijection from the quotient by the kernel relation.

annotations.label: Composition of surjections is surjective

anchor.snippet: The composition of two surjections is again a surjection

annotations.note: Function.Surjective.comp (Lean core, used in Mathlib.Logic.Function.Defs) gives surjectivity of g ∘ f.

annotations.label: Bijection

anchor.snippet: A function is bijective if it is both injective and surjective.

annotations.note: Function.Bijective := Injective f ∧ Surjective f matches this definition verbatim.

annotations.label: Bijective iff invertible

anchor.snippet: is bijective if and only if it is invertible

annotations.note: bijective_iff_has_inverse states Bijective f ↔ ∃ g, LeftInverse g f ∧ RightInverse g f.

annotations.label: Composition of bijections is bijective

anchor.snippet: The composition of two bijections is again a bijection

annotations.note: Function.Bijective.comp proves Bijective g → Bijective f → Bijective (g ∘ f) in Mathlib.Logic.Function.Defs.

annotations.label: Bijections form the symmetric group

anchor.snippet: form a group under composition, called the symmetric group

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm.permGroup is the Group instance on self-bijections Equiv.Perm α, i.e. the symmetric group.

annotations.label: Same cardinality

anchor.snippet: the two sets are said to have the same cardinality

annotations.note: Cardinal.eq states #α = #β ↔ Nonempty (α ≃ β), i.e. equal cardinality iff a bijection exists.

annotations.label: Fewer or equal cardinality via injection

anchor.snippet: has fewer than or the same number of elements

annotations.note: Cardinal.le_def states #α ≤ #β ↔ Nonempty (α ↪ β), capturing ≤ cardinality via an injection/embedding.

annotations.label: Identity function

anchor.snippet: The identity function id X for every non-empty set X

annotations.note: Function.bijective_id proves the identity function is bijective (Mathlib drops the non-emptiness hypothesis).

annotations.label: Exponential restricted to image and logarithm

anchor.snippet: the exponential function with its codomain restricted to its image

annotations.note: Real.expOrderIso : ℝ ≃o Ioi (0:ℝ) is exp with codomain restricted to its image (positive reals), a bijection whose inverse is the logarithm.

annotations.label: Image and preimage inclusions

anchor.snippet: One has always

annotations.note: Set.image_preimage_subset (f '' f ⁻¹' s ⊆ s) and Set.subset_preimage_image (s ⊆ f ⁻¹' f '' s) give both always-holding inclusions.

annotations.label: Surjection-injection decomposition

anchor.snippet: This decomposition as the composition of a surjection and an injection is unique up to an isomorphism

annotations.note: IsImage.isoExt gives uniqueness-up-to-iso of (mono) image factorisations categorically, but the concrete Set-level surjection-then-injection factorization statement is not directly stated.

annotations.label: Correspondence with mono/epi/iso

anchor.snippet: correspond precisely to monomorphisms , epimorphisms , and isomorphisms

annotations.note: Types.mono_iff_injective, Types.epi_iff_surjective, and Types.isIso_iff_bijective establish the correspondence in the category of types.
